metadata.districtModel: five sections; five district slots per section; D13 is the seat of power

cityDNA.coreFantasy: A South African city where Kente Sun Court of the Golden Baobab, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Ancestor Masks Sovereign Registry Court, and the five-section district spine turn local identity into functional civic space.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Khembal should feel immediately identifiable through exact landmarks and materials, not through generic fantasy-city shorthand.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Khembal organizes power through Ancestor Masks Sovereign Registry Court, public response through Covered Bazaar Beacon, civic memory through Kente Sun Court of the Golden Baobab, and daily life through S1 Grand Market Civic Hall, S2 Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 High Residential Civic Hall, S5 University / Lyceum Civic Hall.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Stone, Timber, Iron, Coral Stone, Polished White Limestone, Dry Stone Paving, Civic Terraces, Savanna, Civic Blue Illumination, Oath Seals, Ancestral Spirits, and exact anchors including Ancestor Masks Sovereign Registry Court, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Kente Sun Court of the Golden Baobab, Lecture Hall, Guildhall, Manor Court, Grand Market Civic Hall, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A South African civic culture where architecture, public obligations, roads, landmark approaches, and everyday work must all read as part of the same civilization.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: The city symbol system is built from exact names and visible functions: Kente Sun Court of the Golden Baobab, Covered Bazaar Beacon, Ancestor Masks Sovereign Registry Court, and section anchors such as Grand Market Civic Hall, Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, Records / Archives Hall, High Residential Civic Hall, University / Lyceum Civic Hall.

canonLocks: Khembal uses city code KHB.
The Wonder is Kente Sun Court of the Golden Baobab.
The Beacon is Covered Bazaar Beacon.
The Seat of Power is Ancestor Masks Sovereign Registry Court and occupies District 13.
The city uses five sections with five tactical district slots per section.
The source-backed section spine is: S1 Grand Market Civic Hall, S2 Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 High Residential Civic Hall, S5 University / Lyceum Civic Hall.

rejectedReads: Do not treat Khembal as a generic fantasy settlement with renamed landmarks.
Do not collapse the five-section board into a single mood or single district.
Do not replace exact anchor names with generic summaries.
Do not invent missing street-section landmarks when a street source is absent.
cautionFlags: Keep every named Wonder, Beacon, Seat, section civic hall, major landmark, monument, and minor nook exact when present in sidecars.
If an image appears to deviate from a sidecar, preserve the exact name but avoid overclaiming visual details not supported by the source package.
Every district asset should remain usable for narrative scenes, tactical board dioramas, and city map backgrounds.

environment.environmentalLogic: The environment should make Khembal's exact civic anchors plausible at street scale: terrain routes people toward section landmarks, lighting clarifies public authority, and water or landscape features remain source-backed rather than decorative filler.

streets.appearance: Streets should show Khembal's exact anchor system at pedestrian scale: S1 Grand Market Civic Hall, S2 Artisan Guilds Civic Hall, S3 Records / Archives Hall, S4 High Residential Civic Hall, S5 University / Lyceum Civic Hall. streets.movement: Movement should be readable for tactical-board assets: citizens pass between 25 district slots, section anchors, D13, service streets, and landmark approaches without visual clutter hiding the civic purpose.
sounds: landmark-specific sounds from water, bells, animals, machines, or crowds when source-backed
smells: worn stone, timber, metal, plaster, or fabric
streets.lighting: Use the package's source-backed civic lighting, daylight/golden-hour reads, lanterns, and beacon glow. Lighting must help identify authority, safety, and landmark function.
streets.weatherEffects: Weather should reinforce the local terrain and materials without overwhelming the city identity or inventing unsupported climate extremes.
streets.dayCycle: Day scenes emphasize errands, trade, records, repairs, rituals, food, and section-level movement.
streets.nightCycle: Night scenes emphasize lantern routes, closed thresholds, guarded landmarks, quiet work, and beacon visibility.

common: street services tied to section anchors

housing.wealthy: Prestige housing clusters near Ancestor Masks Sovereign Registry Court, high terraces, or landmark-facing routes, using the best source-backed materials and controlled views of civic space.
housing.middle: Shop-over-house units, courtyard homes, guild-adjacent apartments, and family rooms near section anchors.
housing.poor: Smaller back-street rooms, work-adjacent housing, shared courtyards, service alleys, patched materials, and dense but still city-specific lived-in detail.
